prompt: Write a polite follow-up email after not hearing back about a job application for two weeks.
output:
Subject: Following up on the [Job Title] application

Hi [Name],

Hope your week's going well. I wanted to follow up on my application for the [Job Title] role I submitted on [date]. I'm still very interested in the position and wanted to check whether there's any update on timeline or next steps.

Happy to send over any additional information that might help, references, a portfolio, anything else useful on your end.

Thanks again for considering my application, and I look forward to hearing from you.

Best,
[Name]

prompt: Write a short email declining a meeting invite while keeping the relationship positive.
output:
Subject: Re: Quick sync this week?

Hi [Name],

Thanks for reaching out. I won't be able to make it work this week, my schedule's pretty packed through Friday, but I'd love to find time the week after if that works for you.

If it's time-sensitive, feel free to send over the details by email and I can get back to you with thoughts even without the call.

Let me know what works best.

Best,
[Name]

prompt: Write a short email to a client apologizing for a delayed project delivery.
output:
Subject: Update on the [Project Name] timeline

Hi [Name],

I want to be upfront: the project is running behind schedule, and that's on us. We hit some unexpected issues with [brief reason] that pushed the timeline back further than we initially flagged.

The revised delivery date is [new date], and I'll make sure you get an update before then if anything changes again. I know timing matters on your end, so I don't want to leave you guessing.

Appreciate your patience with this, and happy to jump on a call if it'd help to talk through where things stand.

Best,
[Name]

topic: texting a friend to reschedule plans
prompt: Write a casual text message to a friend asking to reschedule weekend plans.
output: hey, so sorry but something came up this weekend and I can't make Saturday anymore. any chance we could push to next week instead? feel bad bailing last minute, lmk what works for you

topic: checking in on a friend going through a hard time
prompt: Write a short, caring text checking in on a friend who mentioned a tough week.
output: hey, been thinking about you since you mentioned this week's been rough. no need to respond if you're not up for it, just wanted you to know I'm around if you want to talk or even just grab coffee and not talk about it at all

topic: giving constructive feedback to a direct report
prompt: Write a short email giving constructive feedback to a team member about missed deadlines.
output:
Subject: Quick check-in on deadlines

Hi [Name],

Wanted to flag something directly rather than let it sit: the last couple of deliverables have come in later than the agreed dates, and it's starting to create some downstream pressure on the rest of the team.

I know things come up, so I'd rather understand what's going on than just point out the pattern. Is there something making the current workload or timelines harder to hit than expected? Happy to talk through adjusting either if that's the case.

Let's grab fifteen minutes this week to figure out what would help.

Best,
[Name]

prompt: Write a short, professional email negotiating a salary offer.
output:
Subject: Re: Offer for [Job Title]

Hi [Name],

Thank you again for the offer, I'm genuinely excited about the role and the team.

Before accepting, I wanted to discuss the base salary. Based on my research into market rates for this role and my experience with [relevant skill/experience], I was hoping we could explore something closer to [target number]. I'm flexible on how we get there, whether that's base, bonus structure, or other parts of the package.

Let me know if there's room to revisit, and I'm happy to hop on a call if that's easier than going back and forth over email.

Looking forward to hearing your thoughts.

Best,
[Name]

prompt: Write a short, warm text congratulating a friend on a new job.
output: just heard the news, congrats!! so happy for you, you worked hard for this. we need to celebrate properly, let me know when you're free this week

prompt: Write a short email introducing two professional contacts to each other.
output:
Subject: Introduction: [Name A] and [Name B]

Hi both,

Wanted to connect you two. [Name A], [Name B] leads [team/area] at [company] and has a lot of context on [relevant topic]. [Name B], [Name A] is working on [relevant project] and I thought you two would have useful things to compare notes on.

I'll let you take it from here, happy to stay on the thread if useful but otherwise leaving you to it.

Best,
[Name]

prompt: Write a quick casual text coordinating pickup logistics for an event.
output: hey, what time were you thinking for tonight? I can swing by around 7 if that works, or happy to meet there if it's easier for you. let me know
